Package org.eventb.core.ast.tests

Class TestTypeSpecialization

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.AbstractTests
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.TestTypeSpecialization
public class TestTypeSpecialization extends AbstractTests
Unit tests for specialization of types. We check various combinations of types and specialization to ensure that types are correctly substituted.

All tests are of the form of a triplet (original type, specialization, result type).

    void
    bug726()
    Ensures that specializing a type which has no substitution translates it to the factory of the specialization.
    void
    bug727()
    Ensures that specializing a type remembers the types that are not substituted.
    void
    dstIdentBlocksType()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution raises an exception if the type name is already used with a different type in the destination type environment.
    void
    dstIdentDoesNotBlockType()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which gets substituted works as expected, even if the type name is already used with a different type in an identifier substitution.
    void
    dstPredVarDoesNotBlockType()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which gets substituted works as expected, even if the type name is already used with a different type in a predicate variable substitution.
    void
    identBlocksTypeSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution cannot conflict with an existing substitution.
    void
    testBooleanType()
    Ensures that the boolean type is never specialized.
    void
    testGivenType()
    Ensures that a given type can be specialized in various ways.
    void
    testIntegerType()
    Ensures that the integer type is never specialized.
    void
    testParametricType()
    Ensures that parametric types can be specialized in various ways.
    void
    testPowerSetType()
    Ensures that a power set type can be specialized in various ways.
    void
    testPowersetTypeThenRelation()
    Ensures that a relation can't replace a powerset that represents the same type.
    void
    testProductType()
    Ensures that a product type can be specialized in various ways.
    void
    testRelationType()
    Ensures that specializing a type with a relation works.
    void
    testRelationTypeThenPowerset()
    Ensures that if a relation has been put, a powerset expression that represents the same type can't replace it.
    void
    typeBlocksDstIdent()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution using the same identifier in its replacement but with a different type.
    void
    typeBlocksIdent()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the identifier denoting that type.
    void
    typeBlocksIdentDifferentType()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same identifier but with a different type.
    void
    typeBlocksType()
    Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same type.

      Ensures that the integer type is never specialized.

      Ensures that a power set type can be specialized in various ways.

    • testProductType

      public void testProductType()
      Ensures that a product type can be specialized in various ways.

    • testParametricType

      public void testParametricType()
      Ensures that parametric types can be specialized in various ways.

    • bug727

      public void bug727()
      Ensures that specializing a type remembers the types that are not substituted.

    • bug726

      public void bug726()
      Ensures that specializing a type which has no substitution translates it to the factory of the specialization.

    • typeBlocksType

      public void typeBlocksType()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same type.

    • typeBlocksIdent

      public void typeBlocksIdent()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the identifier denoting that type.

    • typeBlocksIdentDifferentType

      public void typeBlocksIdentDifferentType()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same identifier but with a different type.

    • identBlocksTypeSpecialization

      public void identBlocksTypeSpecialization()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution cannot conflict with an existing substitution.

    • typeBlocksDstIdent

      public void typeBlocksDstIdent()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution using the same identifier in its replacement but with a different type.

    • dstIdentBlocksType

      public void dstIdentBlocksType()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which has no substitution raises an exception if the type name is already used with a different type in the destination type environment.

    • dstIdentDoesNotBlockType

      public void dstIdentDoesNotBlockType()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which gets substituted works as expected, even if the type name is already used with a different type in an identifier substitution.

    • dstPredVarDoesNotBlockType

      public void dstPredVarDoesNotBlockType()
      Ensures that specializing a given type which gets substituted works as expected, even if the type name is already used with a different type in a predicate variable substitution.

    • testRelationType

      public void testRelationType()
      Ensures that specializing a type with a relation works. The specificity of relations is that they can be expressed with two different, but equivalent, expressions: T ↔ U and ℙ(T × U). When these type expressions are converted in types, they both yield the type ℙ(T × U). Therefore, specializing a type S with an expression like T ↔ U requires special care to use T ↔ U in expressions and ℙ(T × U) in types.

    • testRelationTypeThenPowerset

      public void testRelationTypeThenPowerset()
      Ensures that if a relation has been put, a powerset expression that represents the same type can't replace it.

    • testPowersetTypeThenRelation

      public void testPowersetTypeThenRelation()
      Ensures that a relation can't replace a powerset that represents the same type.

    • assertSpecialization

      private static void assertSpecialization(String typeImage, String typeSpecializationImage, String expectedImage)

    • assertSpecialization

      private static void assertSpecialization(String typeImage, String typeSpecImage, String expectedImage, FormulaFactory fac)

    • assertSpecialization

      private static void assertSpecialization(ITypeEnvironment srcTypenv, Type srcType, FormulaFactory dstFac, String typeSpecImage, String expectedImage)

    • addGivenSets

      private static void addGivenSets(ITypeEnvironmentBuilder te, Type type)
